steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must join two complete sentences: 'Many works... are [lost]' and 'his Elements... is an extant work.' The word 'Conversely' signals a contrast, so the punctuation must properly separate two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'lost and conversely,': 'and' is a coordinating conjunction but 'conversely' is a conjunctive adverb — combining them creates a grammatically inconsistent hybrid. ✗
  • Choice B — 'lost, conversely,': a comma before a conjunctive adverb joining two independent clauses is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — 'lost. Conversely,': a period ends the first independent clause; 'Conversely,' begins the second with a comma after the conjunctive adverb. This is the correct pattern (IC. Conjunctive adverb, IC). ✓
  • Choice D — 'lost, and conversely': 'and' with a comma can join two independent clauses, but the resulting sentence — 'his Elements... is an extant work' — is not parallel to the first clause and 'conversely' is left dangling without a comma after it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
